toolchain: xcrun --sdk macosx metal/metallib (Apple metal 32023.883 metalfe-32023.883) + swiftc -O

vec_add.kernel: vec_add (FP32 element-wise add, 3*N*4 bytes moved, 1 flop/elem)

honest_scope: First Apple M4 silicon-fire for hexa-lang (mini host, 10-core GPU, 16 GB unified LPDDR5X).
Metal Toolchain not preinstalled on mini — needed `xcodebuild -downloadComponent MetalToolchain` one-time setup. Otherwise toolchain identical to M3 path (xcrun metal/metallib/swiftc -O).
vec_add steady-state (N=1M, N=4M) shows M4 ~100 GB/s vs M3 ~34 GB/s = 2.87-2.97x. Peak (N=256K) 153 GB/s = 4.39x M3.

M3 baseline anchor is rfc075_metal_roofline_2026_05_21 (5 warmup + 50 timed, single Mac local). M4 uses 20+200 reps for tighter variance — measurement methodology delta may inflate ratio slightly, but max|d|=0 byte-eq gate confirms kernel correctness.
simdgroup_matmul_64x64 peak 1858 GFLOPS @ 1024^3 (tg_db variant) vs M3 N37 baseline 1519 = 1.22x; vs M3 N43 follow-up campaign peak 1556 = 1.19x. Modest peak gain.

Biggest M4/M3 matmul delta at 768^3 = 1839/1041 = 1.77x. M3 768^3 was a weak spot (cache/occupancy interaction); M4 closes it. The 911 GFLOPS @ 768^3 reference in the brief is the FP32 N16 baseline; this fire uses mixed-prec N37 protocol where M3 768^3 is 1041.
M4 also closes (-9.4%) at 512^3 (single-buffer M3=908, M4=822), suggesting non-monotonic shape sensitivity remains and M3 had a strong 512 cache hit that M4 doesn't reproduce identically.
Cross-host comparison: M3 fire on shared Mac developer laptop (variable thermal/load); M4 fire on freshly-booted mini with no concurrent load. M3 variance was 'noisier than dedicated GPU host'.

max_abs_diff=0 across all 12 matmul rows AND all 4 vec-add rows: deterministic numeric output, no FP rounding drift in MMA fast-path on M4 vs M3 (same Metal 32023.883 compiler version, same kernel source).

Apple M4 advertised spec: ~4.6 TFLOPS FP32 / 120 GB/s LPDDR5X-7500 (16 GB variant). M4 measured 1858 GFLOPS mixed-prec = ~40% of advertised FP32 peak. M3 N37 1519 GFLOPS = ~43% of M3's 3.5 TFLOPS spec. Both leave ~60% on the table to MPS/tensor-tuned codepaths.

Matmul ratio cluster: 1.22-1.77x across shapes, median ~1.5x. Consistent with M3->M4 architecture: more cores (8->10 = 1.25x), faster LPDDR5X (1.17x), unchanged MMA intrinsic throughput per simdgroup.
